title: phlox drummondii subsp. mcallisteri
intro: Phlox drummondii subsp. mcallisteri, also known as Drummond's phlox or Texas phlox, is a flowering plant native to North America, specifically in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phlox drummondii subsp. mcallisteri include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Phlox drummondii subsp. mcallisteri can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
